sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Union Pacific earnings up next as fuel costs, merger scrutiny loom By Investing.com
feed.summary: Union Pacific is set to report its first-quarter earnings, with analysts expecting growth despite challenges from rising fuel costs and regulatory scrutiny over its proposed merger with Norfolk Southern. Investors will be closely watching operating efficiency, volume trends, pricing power, and any updates on the merger's approval timeline, especially under stricter 2001 regulations. The company aims to sustain operational gains from 2025 while navigating these near-term pressures and complex regulatory hurdles.

feed.title: United Airlines says fares may need to rise up to 20% to offset fuel surge
feed.summary: United Airlines CEO Scott Kirby stated that ticket prices might need to increase by 15% to 20% to absorb rising jet fuel costs, despite robust demand for premium travel. The airline aims to fully recover these costs, targeting double-digit pre-tax margins next year, and has already begun implementing fare increases and higher baggage fees. While demand has not yet dropped, Kirby acknowledged that continued price hikes will eventually test consumer willingness to pay.

feed.title: Delta and United benefit from competitors cutting capacity amid higher fuel costs – analyst (DAL:NYSE)
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines and United Airlines are set to benefit as lower-margin competitors reduce capacity due to higher fuel costs, solidifying their positions as dominant "premium carriers." Both airlines anticipate flat ticket prices in Q4, with projected increases of 6-7% in Q1 and potentially double-digit growth in Q2, allowing them to pass rising fuel costs to consumers. Additionally, Boeing's production rates and aircraft price increases support its positive cash flow outlook.

feed.title: Globe Life GL Q3 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Globe Life (GL) reported strong Q3 2025 results with double-digit growth in net income and net operating income, driven by premium and underwriting margin expansion. The company emphasized investments in agent recruiting and technology, aiming for significant agent force and sales growth by 2030, and returned substantial capital to shareholders through buybacks, supported by an extraordinary dividend. Guidance for 2026 projects continued premium and underwriting margin gains, with a new Bermuda affiliate expected to boost cash flow in future periods.

feed.title: Horizon Kinetics buys Texas Pacific Land stock, $441
feed.summary: Horizon Kinetics Asset Management LLC reported buying shares of Texas Pacific Land Corp (NYSE:TPL) valued at $441 on April 21, 2026, at $441.84 per share. This purchase increased their direct holdings to 3,435,501 shares, solidifying their position as a 10% owner of the company. The acquisition comes amidst recent news of the passing of board member Murray Stahl and KeyBanc reiterating an "Overweight" rating with a price target of $639 due to TPL's power generation and data center opportunities.

feed.title: Raymond James Upgrades Axos Financial to Strong Buy Status
feed.summary: Raymond James has upgraded Axos Financial (AX) to a Strong Buy rating with a $100 price target, citing sustained confidence in the company's long-term organic growth and premium profitability. This upgrade follows Axos Bank's recognition by S&P Global as a top-performing publicly traded U.S. bank for 2025 among institutions with over $10 billion in assets. The company continues to be recognized as one of the fastest-growing financial stocks, driven by its digital-first approach and diverse financial services.

feed.title: CT electric bills are coming down. Rate cut will save Eversouce, UI customers up to $34 monthly
feed.summary: Connecticut electric customers are set to see their bills decrease starting May 1, with Eversource customers saving $30 monthly and United Illuminating (UI) customers saving $34 monthly due to a rate adjustment. This reduction, approved by the Public Utilities Regulatory Authority (PURA), is largely driven by lower public benefits charges linked to nuclear power contracts. Governor Ned Lamont highlighted that these savings are a direct result of previous decisions, including restructuring utility arrearages and investing in nuclear power.

feed.title: Micron lobbies US Congress to pass law restricting chipmaking tool exports to China: report
feed.summary: Micron Technology is supporting the proposed MATCH Act in the US Congress, which aims to tighten restrictions on semiconductor manufacturing equipment exports to China by US allies. The legislation seeks to close gaps in current export controls to curb China's advanced memory and semiconductor development for national security reasons. However, the bill's broad scope could challenge transatlantic alliances and diplomatic relations, particularly with European suppliers like ASML.

feed.title: Moody’s upgrades 5 bond classes backed by Axos unit’s equipment loans
feed.summary: Moody’s Ratings has upgraded five classes of notes across Verdant Receivables’ 2023-1 and 2024-1 equipment asset-backed securitizations, a division of Axos Bank. These upgrades reflect strong collateral performance, increasing credit enhancement, and the stability of Verdant’s equipment finance platform. The agency highlighted sequential pay structures, non-declining reserve accounts, and growing overcollateralization as key drivers, along with sustained collateral outperformance.

feed.title: Fund Update: 25,606 REGENERON PHARMACEUTICALS (REGN) shares added to WestEnd Advisors, LLC portfolio
feed.summary: WestEnd Advisors, LLC has increased its holdings in Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN) by adding 25,606 shares to its portfolio, as revealed in a recent SEC 13F filing for the Q1 2026 report period. The article also details significant movements by other institutional investors, insider trading activity (primarily sales), and Congressional stock trading (purchases) related to REGN. Wall Street analysts maintain a positive outlook on REGN, with multiple "Buy" or "Overweight" ratings and a median price target of $860.0.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Lawsuit Puts Culture Governance And Valuation In Investor Focus
feed.summary: A former Wells Fargo advisor has filed an age discrimination lawsuit against the bank, alleging that older employees were not given the same progressive discipline as younger staff. This lawsuit introduces new legal and reputational concerns for Wells Fargo, shifting investor focus beyond recent earnings and margins to culture and governance. Investors are advised to monitor Wells Fargo's response to the lawsuit, potential changes in HR policies, and regulatory scrutiny, as these issues could impact management focus, operational priorities, and future risk disclosures.

feed.title: Freeport-McMoRan earnings on deck: Grasberg timeline in focus By Investing.com
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan is set to release its first-quarter earnings, with analysts expecting strong growth bolstered by higher copper prices and operational improvements. Investors will be primarily focused on the company's guidance for the phased restart of its Grasberg Block Cave mine in Indonesia, a critical high-margin asset, which was curtailed due to a mudslide. The company aims for 85% production recovery by the end of 2026.

feed.title: Assessing Edison International (EIX) Valuation After Recent Share Price Pullback
feed.summary: Edison International (EIX) has seen a recent share price pullback, leading investors to re-evaluate its valuation against fundamentals. While one narrative suggests the stock is 6% undervalued with a fair value of $74.19 due to anticipated load growth and grid modernization, a Simply Wall St discounted cash flow model indicates an overvaluation with a fair value of $37.30. This discrepancy highlights mixed sentiment and the importance for investors to conduct their own analysis, considering potential risks like wildfire liabilities and future rate decisions.

feed.title: Crown Castle (CCI) Q1 2025 Earnings Transcript
feed.summary: Crown Castle (CCI) leadership confirmed their strategic progression toward becoming a pure-play U.S. tower company, with the Fiber and small cell divestment on track for a first-half 2026 closing after regulatory reviews. The company reiterated its 2025 outlook for organic tower growth, adjusted EBITDA, and AFFO, maintaining longer-term AFFO targets post-separation. Capital allocation plans, including a dividend reduction, share repurchase program, and debt repayment, align with previously announced frameworks, emphasizing balance sheet strength and an investment-grade credit rating.

feed.title: Charleston PD investigating after reported robbery attempt at King Street bank
feed.summary: Charleston Police are investigating a reported robbery attempt that occurred at the Huntington Bank on King Street on April 21st. An individual allegedly entered the bank, demanded money, and then fled the scene. No arrests have been made and the incident remains under active investigation.

feed.title: Moderna, after losing US funding, rebounds to start mRNA bird flu vaccine trial
feed.summary: Moderna has initiated a Phase 3 trial for its mRNA bird flu vaccine, mRNA-1018, despite losing significant U.S. government funding. The trial aims to enroll 4,000 healthy adults in the U.S. and U.K. to assess the vaccine's safety and immune response. This move follows a period where the Trump administration canceled contracts for mRNA research, but the Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations stepped in to provide up to $54.3 million in funding for the vaccine's development.

feed.title: All eyes on Las Vegas Sands earnings amid Macau margin worries
feed.summary: Las Vegas Sands is preparing to report its first-quarter earnings, with investors closely watching its profit margins, especially regarding its strategic shift to premium customers in Macau. Analysts forecast solid growth but anticipate a sequential dip from the previous quarter's strong performance. The success of this high-spending, but potentially lower-margin, segment in Macau, along with the continued performance of Marina Bay Sands, will be key areas of focus.

feed.title: All eyes on Raymond James earnings amid peer outperformance By Investing.com
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial Inc. is set to report its fiscal second-quarter results, with investors keenly watching to see if the wealth management firm can match the strong performance of its peers. Analysts project 17% year-over-year EPS growth and 13% revenue growth, though sequential EPS is expected to decline slightly from the prior quarter. The focus will be on margin dynamics, expense management, and wealth management trends in a competitive environment.

feed.title: Crown Castle earnings on deck as tower growth concerns linger By Investing.com
feed.summary: Crown Castle International Corp. is set to release its first-quarter earnings, with investors focused on the company's transformation into a pure-play U.S. infrastructure company and its organic tower growth rates. Analysts project earnings of $0.38 per share on $995 million in revenue, while concerns persist about Crown Castle's organic growth lagging behind competitors. The company's plan to sell its fiber and small cell businesses and use proceeds for debt repayment and share repurchases will also be a key focus.

feed.title: Scotiabank Adjusts ConocoPhillips Price Target to $125 From $100, Maintains Sector Perform Rating
feed.summary: Scotiabank has increased its price target for ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) to $125 from $100, while reiterating a Sector Perform rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects an updated outlook from the financial institution on the oil and gas exploration and production company. ConocoPhillips' stock was trading at $122.32, showing a change of +1.71% in the last 5 days and +30.47% since January 1st.

feed.title: Scotiabank Adjusts Price Target on Coterra Energy to $32 From $31, Maintains Sector Perform Rating
feed.summary: Scotiabank has increased its price target for Coterra Energy (CTRA) to $32 from $31, while reiterating a "Sector Perform" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects the ongoing analysis of the oil and natural gas exploration and production company, which specializes in operations across North America. The report highlights Scotiabank's current outlook on Coterra Energy's financial prospects and market position.

feed.title: Booking Holdings Inc Stock (BKNG) Moved Down by 3.91% on Apr 22: What Investors Need To Know
feed.summary: Booking Holdings Inc. (BKNG) shares fell by 3.91% due to regulatory scrutiny over Booking.com's marketing practices in Italy, analyst downgrades following a stock split, and upcoming Q1 2026 earnings. Weakening business travel sentiment, recent insider selling, and technical indicators suggesting oversold conditions further contributed to the decline. The company also faces ongoing antitrust challenges in Europe and recently experienced a data breach, adding to investor concerns.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc Stock (AMD) Moved Up by 3.41% on Apr 22: What Investors Need To Know
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc (AMD) saw its stock price increase by 3.41% on April 22nd, driven by the launch of its new Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 dual-cache processor and positive analyst revisions based on strong demand in the AI sector. Analysts have raised price targets for AMD, citing AI-driven compute demand exceeding expectations and significant customer commitments. The company's data center segment and MI400 series AI accelerators are expected to be key growth drivers, with upcoming Q1 2026 financial results highly anticipated.

feed.title: Seagate Technology Holdings PLC Stock (STX) Moved Up by 3.04% on Apr 22: What Investors Need To Know
feed.summary: Seagate Technology Holdings PLC (STX) stock rose 3.04% on April 22, driven by positive analyst upgrades, including Barclays moving it to Overweight, and strong industry dynamics related to AI-driven demand and HAMR technology. The company's robust financial performance and "sold-out" HDD capacity through 2026 further boosted investor confidence, despite concerns about its valuation and insider selling.

feed.title: Public Storage (PSA) director converts LTIP and AO LTIP units into 6,186 share equivalents
feed.summary: Public Storage director Avedick Baruyr Poladian converted LTIP and AO LTIP units into 6,186 common share equivalents on April 20, 2026. This transaction involved the exercise of 1,023.44 LTIP Units and 5,163 AO LTIP Units, with the AO LTIP Units fully exercised at a conversion price of $250.29 per unit. The conversion is a routine compensation-related equity conversion and does not represent open-market sales.

feed.title: Newmont Corporation Stock (NEM) Moved Up by 3.00% on Apr 22: A Full Analysis
feed.summary: Newmont Corporation's stock (NEM) rose by 3.00% today, outpacing the Mineral Resources sector, driven by a rebound in gold prices due to safe-haven demand and geopolitical tensions. Positive analyst upgrades and anticipation of strong first-quarter earnings, despite some production concerns, also contributed to the upward movement. Technical indicators show a buy signal with a MACD value of [0.62], though RSI is neutral and Williams %R suggests an oversold condition.

feed.title: P&G Beats Coffee Co's Bid To Nix Pepto-Bismol TM
feed.summary: European officials have ruled against a Spanish café owner's attempt to revoke Procter & Gamble's "Pepto" trademark. The ruling stated that consumers would not confuse the coffee products of "Cafes Pepetto" with Pepto-Bismol medication, as the products are unrelated. This decision protects P&G's trademark rights for its popular indigestion remedy.

feed.title: Dollar General marks Earth Day with Arbor Day Foundation milestone
feed.summary: Dollar General celebrates Earth Day by highlighting its five-year partnership with the Arbor Day Foundation, which has led to significant reforestation and community recovery efforts. The collaboration has resulted in over 434,000 trees planted across the U.S. and Mexico, restoring more than 848 acres of forest. These initiatives underscore Dollar General's commitment to sustainability and corporate responsibility, integrating environmental impact reduction with community engagement.

feed.title: Scotiabank Adjusts Price Target on Occidental Petroleum to $57 From $46, Maintains Sector Perform Rating
feed.summary: Scotiabank has increased its price target for Occidental Petroleum (OXY) to $57 from $46, while maintaining a "Sector Perform" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a more optimistic outlook on the company's valuation by the bank. Occidental Petroleum is a major oil and gas exploration and production company, with its primary operations in the United States.

feed.title: BMO Capital Adjusts Price Target on Sempra to $105 From $106, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: BMO Capital has updated its price target for Sempra, lowering it from $106 to $105, while reiterating an "Outperform" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a revised valuation perspective for the utility company. The article also notes other recent analyst rating changes for Sempra, including those from Morgan Stanley, Wells Fargo, JPMorgan, Jefferies, and Barclays.

feed.title: Is Las Vegas Sands Big Macao Push Going to Hurt?
feed.summary: Las Vegas Sands (LVS) is facing margin pressure in Macao despite beating Q4 2025 revenue estimates. The company targets $700 million in quarterly Macao EBITDA but is challenged by higher promotional spending and wage inflation, particularly with its focus on premium mass and rolling segments. Investors are keenly watching the Q1 2026 results for signs of Macao margin improvement and the sustainability of strong performance from its Marina Bay Sands property in Singapore.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Adjusts Price Target on Teradyne to $376 From $306, Maintains Equalweight Rating
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has raised its price target for Teradyne (TER) shares to $376 from $306, while maintaining an Equalweight rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects an updated outlook for the semiconductor equipment and testing company. The news was published by MT Newswires on April 22, 2026.

feed.title: BNP Paribas Adjusts Steel Dynamics Price Target to $238 From $218, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: BNP Paribas has raised its price target for Steel Dynamics (STLD) to $238 from $218, while reiterating an Outperform rating. This adjustment reflects a positive outlook on the steel producer. The news comes alongside similar price target adjustments from other financial institutions, including JPMorgan, BMO Capital, Citigroup, Wells Fargo, and KeyBanc, predominantly maintaining positive ratings or adjusting targets upwards.

feed.title: Masco stock holds at Hold as Jefferies keeps $73 price target
feed.summary: Jefferies maintained a Hold rating and a $73 price target for Masco (NYSE:MAS) after the company reported first-quarter results that surpassed Wall Street expectations, with EBITDA and EPS exceeding consensus estimates. Despite the strong performance, Masco reiterated its full-year guidance, acknowledging macroeconomic and geopolitical uncertainties, leading Jefferies to maintain its rating. InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock appears overvalued relative to its Fair Value assessment.

feed.title: Barclays (DJP) offers buffered callable contingent coupon notes linked to XME and COPX
feed.summary: Barclays Bank PLC is offering Buffered Callable Contingent Coupon Notes due April 27, 2028, linked to the performance of the SPDR S&P Metals & Mining ETF (XME) and the Global X Copper Miners ETF (COPX). These notes offer contingent coupons of $12.083 per $1,000 principal amount if both underlying ETFs meet their 70% coupon barriers on specified observation dates, but expose investors to significant principal loss if the least performing ETF falls below a 30% buffer. The investment carries substantial credit risk from Barclays Bank PLC, including the potential exercise of U.K. bail-in powers.

feed.title: Applied Materials Just Joined the Tesla Terafab Project. Does That Make AMAT Stock a Buy?
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) has joined Tesla's Terafab project, where Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI are reportedly seeking quotes for semiconductor equipment to support their domestic chipmaking efforts. While this connection highlights AMAT's critical role in next-gen chip manufacturing, the Terafab project is in early stages with no confirmed orders yet. AMAT has seen significant stock appreciation, riding the AI rally, boasting strong Q1 earnings, and receiving bullish projections from analysts despite high current valuations.

feed.title: Congressional Trading Report: Rep. Maria Elvira Salazar Bought Over $221K In Amgen Stock
feed.summary: Representative Maria Elvira Salazar purchased Amgen stock valued between $221,025 and $865,000 on March 24, 2026, as per a report filed on April 21, 2026. This transaction is part of her active trading history, which includes 44 stock transactions totaling over $3.42 million in the last three years. Congressional transactions like these are publicly disclosed under the STOCK Act and can offer insights for investors, though they should not be the sole basis for investment decisions.

feed.title: ArcBest adds Scope 3 emissions data to its annual impact report
feed.summary: ArcBest (Nasdaq: ARCB) has released its 2025 Impact Report, which includes the company's first-ever disclosure of certain Scope 3 greenhouse gas emissions, alongside its existing Scope 1 and Scope 2 reporting. The report covers business-relevant environmental, operational, and workforce topics, with Scope 3 categories including Purchased Goods and Services (Purchased Transportation only), Capital Expenditures, Fuel- and Energy-Related Activities, and Business Travel. This marks the seventh annual impact report for the integrated logistics company.

feed.title: Record 2025 results and $4.20 dividend in Garmin (NYSE: GRMN) proxy
feed.summary: Garmin's proxy statement details a proposed $4.20 per share cash dividend for 2025, payable in four equal installments, supported by record financial results including $7.25 billion in revenue and $1.88 billion in operating income. Shareholders will vote on 14 proposals at the June 5, 2026 annual meeting, including director re-elections, executive and board compensation limits, and the appointment of Ernst & Young as auditors. The report also highlights Garmin's corporate governance, compensation practices, and sustainability efforts.

feed.title: Globe Life earnings ahead: Can it deliver on raised guidance?
feed.summary: Globe Life Inc. is set to announce its first-quarter earnings, which will be a critical indicator of its ability to meet the ambitious full-year guidance raised just two months ago. Analysts anticipate earnings of $3.48 per share on $1.56 billion in revenue, with a focus on normalizing mortality trends, the growth of its exclusive agent force, and consistent execution towards its full-year targets. The company's stock currently trades at a forward P/E ratio of 9.8, below historical norms, despite analysts projecting a 15.2% return on equity for 2026.

feed.title: San Jose food orders can now arrive by sidewalk robot on Uber Eats
feed.summary: Uber Eats has partnered with Coco Robotics to launch autonomous, zero-emission robot food deliveries in downtown San Jose, California, effective April 22, 2026. Coco's robots, which have completed over 500,000 deliveries, will serve the city's core, aiming to assist restaurants with efficient order fulfillment and provide customers with fast, reliable, and environmentally friendly delivery options. This initiative expands Uber's automation strategy across urban markets and leverages Coco's extensive operational data for rapid adaptation and scaling.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Lent to UK’s MFS as Barclays Exited Deal, Froze Accounts
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Co. stepped in to lend £143 million ($193 million) to a company associated with Market Financial Solutions Ltd. (MFS) in late November. This occurred shortly after Barclays Plc began freezing MFS's accounts and subsequently exited its lending arrangement with the now-collapsed mortgage firm. Barclays was reportedly repaid a similar amount as a result of this transaction.

feed.title: BlackBerry Stock Hits New 52-Week High Amid Strategic AI Pivot
feed.summary: BlackBerry (NYSE: BB) stock reached a new 52-week high, driven by its strategic pivot into AI and automotive software via its QNX unit. The company's QNX is integrating with Nvidia IGX Thor for safety-certified AI systems and was selected by Leapmotor for its upcoming D19 electric SUV. Despite strong momentum and growth potential, BlackBerry faces a "Weak" valuation score and an overbought RSI, suggesting potential for sharper pullbacks if sentiment shifts.

feed.title: AutoCallable Oracle‑linked Notes from Barclays Bank PLC (DJP) due May 2027
feed.summary: Barclays Bank PLC is offering AutoCallable Contingent Coupon Notes due May 10, 2027, linked to Oracle Corporation's common stock, with an initial issue price of $1,000 per note and an estimated value between $912.80 and $962.80. These notes offer a contingent coupon of $30.75 per $1,000 if Oracle's closing value meets or exceeds the Coupon Barrier on specified Observation Dates, and can be automatically redeemed if the stock reaches a Call Value. Investors face the risk of losing up to 100% of their principal if Oracle's final value falls below the Barrier, and payments are subject to Barclays' creditworthiness and potential U.K. Bail-in Power.

feed.title: Starbucks cuts jobs in technology teams
feed.summary: Starbucks has announced staff cuts within its technology organization as part of a restructuring effort to move faster and sharpen focus, according to an internal communication. The company declined to disclose the exact number of layoffs or confirm if they were Seattle-based roles. This move comes as Starbucks undergoes a major turnaround, including investments in store upgrades and cost-cutting measures, under CEO Brian Niccol, who joined in 2024.

feed.title: Axe Compute jumps nearly 96% after unveiling a $260M enterprise NVIDIA B300 GPU deal
feed.summary: Axe Compute Inc. (AGPU) saw its stock jump nearly 96% after announcing a 36-month, $260 million enterprise infrastructure contract to deliver 2,304 NVIDIA B300 GPUs. This significant deal, structured with take-or-pay payments, suggests a substantial increase in booked revenue visibility for the company's "neocloud" model. Hedge funds have shown increased interest, with 15 institutional investors adding AGPU shares in the last quarter.

feed.title: Hicks Family Trust sells 4,028 JBHT shares (NASDAQ: JBHT) in Form 144 filing
feed.summary: The Hicks Family Trust has filed a Form 144, indicating proposed sales of J.B. Hunt Transport Services (NASDAQ: JBHT) common stock. The filing details two recent dispositions: 4,028 shares sold on February 4, 2026, for $906,828.16, and 3,919 shares sold on January 22, 2026, for $823,512.11. The shares were acquired through equity compensation and are traded on Nasdaq.

feed.title: Qorvo (QRVO) and Skyworks Solutions (SWKS) Upgraded by Barclays
feed.summary: Barclays upgraded Qorvo Inc and Skyworks Solutions to Overweight, increasing their price targets, citing benefits from Apple's new product strategies including foldable iPhones. Qorvo's GF Score of 76/100 indicates strong long-term potential, despite some insider selling and challenges in growth, while its P/E ratio is near a 3-year low. This upgrade reflects confidence in the company's ability to adapt to evolving semiconductor market trends.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs (GSCE) $10M callable notes pay 5.00% through 2030
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs is offering $10 million in Callable Fixed Rate Notes due October 22, 2030, paying 5.00% annual interest. The company retains the option to redeem these notes quarterly starting October 22, 2026. These notes are issued at 100% of principal with an underwriting concession of up to 0.15% and are subject to FATCA withholding rules.

feed.title: International Flavors & Fragrances (NYSE:IFF) Price Target Lowered to $88.00 at Oppenheimer
feed.summary: Oppenheimer has lowered its price target for International Flavors & Fragrances (NYSE:IFF) to $88.00 from $97.00, while maintaining an "outperform" rating. This adjustment comes after IFF missed its Q4 EPS estimates and reported a 6.6% year-over-year revenue decrease. Despite the lowered target, an insider, Paul J. Fribourg, recently purchased 142,000 shares, indicating confidence in the company.
